# Phandalin Gazetteer

World info for the frontier village of Phandalin and its immediate
surroundings. No Adventure structure — this is Reference-note material
that any Adventure set in the area can draw on.

## The village

Phandalin sits at the foot of the Sword Mountains, on the ruins of a much
older settlement destroyed centuries ago. Roughly 50 permanent residents,
swelling with prospectors and caravan crews in the warmer months. No
standing guard; townmaster Harbin Wester is widely considered a coward.

## People worth knowing

- **Sera** — village blacksmith. Inherited the forge from her father.
  Knew the prior owners of the Wave Echo Cave operation and is one of
  the few residents who remembers Phandalin from before the Redbrand
  takeover. Reliable source of local rumor; sympathetic to outsiders
  who treat the village seriously.
- **Toblen Stonehill** — innkeeper of the Stonehill Inn. Earnest, low
  on information, useful for paid rooms and a hot meal.
- **Daran Edermath** — retired adventurer, ranger, runs the orchard
  east of town. Will quietly assist parties he judges competent.

## Factions present in Phandalin

- **The Redbrands** — a thug operation working out of Tresendar Manor's
  cellars. They extort the shops, including Sera's forge. Known to the
  villagers; nobody has been able to drive them out.

## Locations adjacent to the village

- **Tresendar Manor** — ruined manor on the village outskirts; Redbrand
  base of operations.
- **The Sleeping Giant** — tap-house favoured by the Redbrands; not
  somewhere the village locals drink.
- **Wave Echo Cave** — abandoned mine a day's travel into the Sword
  Mountains. Source of the village's older wealth; long thought lost.
